title: Systematic falsification: OHLCV-only intraday signals (ORB, gaps, liquidity grabs) leave 0.07-1.50 pts/trade gross on MNQ — none pass all criteria
claim: On MNQ 5-minute data 2021-2025 (947 trading days), a systematic test of opening range breakouts, gap strategies, volume signals, cross-session momentum, liquidity grabs, and volatility-conditioned classifiers found gross next-bar-open edge of only ~0.07-1.50 points per trade, and no signal satisfied all robustness criteria simultaneously.

source.label: arXiv 2605.04004 — Structural Limits of OHLCV-Based Intraday Signals in MNQ Futures: A Systematic Falsification Study
source.quote: The gross edge available to next-bar-open execution is constrained to approximately 0.07-1.50 points per trade ... A gap-continuation signal achieves T = 3.23 and +14.52 points but fails minimum sample requirements (N = 22) ... No signal satisfies all criteria simultaneously

rule_formal: Falsification protocol: signal families (ORB, gap, volume, cross-session momentum, liquidity grab, vol-conditioned classifier) -> next-bar-open execution -> require min sample N + T-stat + net-of-cost expectancy simultaneously. Individual signal rules in full PDF (not extracted).

numbers.effect: gross edge 0.07-1.50 points/trade; best signal (gap continuation) +14.52 pts/trade, T=3.23, but N=22 fails sample minimum; zero signals pass all criteria
numbers.caveats: index futures, not gold — XAUUSD session structure (Asian range) differs; 5-min not M1; abstract-level extraction (full PDF is image-encoded, not text-extracted); falsification framing means absence of edge in THEIR rule variants, not all variants

mechanism: Claim is structural: information content of OHLCV bars alone (without order-flow/DOM) is too small relative to spread+slippage at intraday horizons on a liquid index future.
relevance: Q1 direct and adversarial (liquidity grabs and breakout signals from bars alone were tested and failed on MNQ — sets a hostile prior for any Q1/Q4 candidate: demand it clear cost-adjusted, minimum-sample bars), Q4 (their 'liquidity grab' family is the formalized sweep)

test_design.lookahead_risk: their protocol (next-bar-open execution) is exactly the anti-lookahead discipline our sim uses — adoptable as-is

overlap_risk: their volatility-conditioned classifier family overlaps rv percentile (falsified — consistent with their null result); gap family overlaps overnight gap ratio (falsified — again consistent)
